summary: Surah Al-Qiyamah swears by the Day of Resurrection and the self-reproaching soul, affirming Allah’s power to reassemble bones and resurrect. It instructs the Prophet not to hasten his tongue with the Qur’an—its collection, recitation, and explanation are upon Allah. People love the fleeting world and neglect the Hereafter; faces will be radiant, looking toward their Lord, while others will be grim, anticipating crushing torment. The surah concludes by recalling human creation from a drop to male and female as proof that Allah can surely revive the dead.
